name: David Frost
description: British television host, journalist, comedian and writer (1939–2013)
abstract: Sir David Paradine Frost (7 April 1939 – 31 August 2013) was an English television host, journalist, comedian, and writer. He rose to prominence during the satire boom in the United Kingdom when he was chosen to host the satirical programme That Was the Week That Was in 1962.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Of course I worked with him whenever he came out here to California with the big band. What was it, the David Frost Show? I would always do the show with him out here.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions working for the 'David Frost Show' and notes that 'David Frost was 29, so he was making millions of dollars a year at that time,' but describes no personal interaction or relationship with David Frost himself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Taylor, Dr. Billy Taylor, afforded me the opportunity to arrange — compose and arrange — some music for a show he was the musical director of at that time, that was David Frost.
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I was watching last night — I don’t know what station, but I got Wynton Marsalis doing an interview with David Frost.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: in getting ready for the pilot they showed us tapes of this English guy who was funny. His name was David Frost.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
